# AI Eco Footprint

> A free, client-side web calculator that estimates the environmental cost of any AI prompt — electricity usage, water consumption, and CO₂ emissions — using peer-reviewed per-token baselines and regional grid multipliers. No account or data collection.

## What this tool does

The calculator accepts any AI prompt as text input, estimates its token count (words × 1.33), and multiplies by three research-validated baselines to produce single-prompt impact and annualised projections. A secondary live counter shows aggregate global AI resource use, seeded to the current time of day, updated every 250 ms.

### Environmental baselines (per token)

- **Electricity:** 0.00045 Wh — source: Luccioni, Jernite & Strubell 2023 (arXiv:2311.16863)
- **Water:** 0.004 mL — source: Li, Yang, Islam & Ren 2023 (arXiv:2304.03271)
- **Carbon:** 0.0002 g CO₂e — source: International Energy Agency (IEA), Data Centres & Energy and AI reports

### Regional multipliers

Results are adjusted by a user-selected compute region:

| Region | Electricity | Water | Carbon |
|---|---|---|---|
| Global Grid Average (default) | 1.00× | 1.00× | 1.00× |
| US East — Northern Virginia | 1.25× | 1.20× | 1.25× |
| US West — Oregon | 0.50× | 0.80× | 0.50× |
| Europe — France (nuclear-dominant) | 0.15× | 1.10× | 0.15× |
| Europe — Ireland (wind + gas) | 0.85× | 0.90× | 0.85× |

### Human-scale equivalents

- Electricity → iPhone 17 full charges (14.5 Wh per charge)
- Water → standard 500 mL water bottles
- Carbon → miles driven in a gasoline sedan (~200 g CO₂e per mile)

### Scope and caveats

Values are order-of-magnitude estimates using averaged peer-reviewed baselines. Actual impact varies with model architecture, server utilisation, workload scheduling, and data center cooling configuration. These results are not a certified measurement tool.

## Key pages

- [Calculator](https://www.aiecofootprint.org/): Main interactive tool — prompt input, regional multiplier selector, daily usage slider, impact cards, and global debt clock.

## Research sources

- [Luccioni, Jernite & Strubell 2023 — Power Hungry Processing (arXiv:2311.16863)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.16863): Peer-reviewed electricity per-token baseline, published via Hugging Face & ACM FAccT.
- [Li, Yang, Islam & Ren 2023 — Making AI Less Thirsty (arXiv:2304.03271)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.03271): Peer-reviewed water per-token baseline, University of California, Riverside / Communications of the ACM.
- [IEA — Data Centres and Data Transmission Networks](https://www.iea.org/energy-system/buildings/data-centres-and-data-transmission-networks): Carbon and grid intensity baseline.
- [IEA — Energy and AI (2024)](https://www.iea.org/reports/energy-and-ai): Carbon and grid intensity baseline.

## Optional

- [README / Methodology & Contributing](https://github.com/jamirand09-web/aiecofootprint.org/blob/main/README.md): Detailed assumptions, regional multiplier rationale, and how to contribute more granular data.
- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/jamirand09-web/aiecofootprint.org/issues): Open an issue to suggest refined baselines or regional data.
